## Overview

**v0.4.2 — bug fixes + rename UX polish.**

A small follow-up to v0.4.1 that closes four post-release issues found
during user testing. No schema changes, no migration. The headline fix
is the macOS multi-tab mouse-selection regression: in v0.4.1, with two
or more tabs open, terminal-area click / drag / double-click silently
did nothing (the `.none` branch of the tab-bar hit-area dispatch ate
every event below the tab bar). The other three close out tab-rename /
selection lifecycle work that should have landed alongside v0.4.1 but
slipped through.

## Downloads

| Platform | File | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Windows | `tildaz-v0.4.2-windows.zip` | Unzip anywhere. Includes `tildaz.exe` + bundled ConPTY runtime. Unsigned — same AV / SmartScreen handling as v0.4.1. |
| macOS (universal) | `tildaz-v0.4.2-macos.dmg` | Apple Silicon + Intel single binary. Drag `TildaZ.app` into Applications. macOS 13 Ventura or later. |

First-launch on macOS: same ad-hoc-signing setup as v0.4.1 — right-click
`TildaZ.app` → Open, or `xattr -d com.apple.quarantine
/Applications/TildaZ.app`. No re-grant of permissions if you already
ran v0.4.1.

## Migration from v0.4.1

None. Same config schema, same defaults.

## What's new since v0.4.1

### Bug fixes

- **macOS multi-tab terminal selection silently broken**
  ([#172](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/172)). With two or
  more tabs open, mouse drag / double-click in the terminal area did
  nothing. The `tildazMouseDown` multi-tab branch was treating the
  `.none` hit-area result as "tab-bar area but no specific button" and
  returning, but `tab_layout.hitArea` actually returns `.none` whenever
  `py >= tab_bar_h` — i.e. *every* click below the tab bar. The result
  was a 100% deterministic miss whenever `g_session.count() >= 2`.
  Originally reported as a stochastic "1/10 stuck" bug; turned out to
  be fully deterministic, single-tab vs. multi-tab. Fix: the `.none`
  switch arm falls through to the cell-selection / double-click code
  below.
- **Config `_` prefix keys rejected by strict schema validation**
  ([#173](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/173)). The schema
  fatals on unknown keys by design, which broke a common JSON
  comment-by-convention (`"_note": "..."`). Now keys starting with `_`
  are skipped from validation entirely (type + recurse). The official
  schema never uses `_` so there's no collision. CONFIG.md gains a
  *Comment keys* section explaining the convention and a temporary-
  disable trick (`"shell"` → `"_shell"`).

### Cross-platform parity follow-ups (post-v0.4.1)

- **Per-tab terminal-interaction state on Windows + macOS
  `cancelPointerModes` parity + β policy on tab switch**
  ([#174](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/174)). The Windows
  `App.terminal_interaction` global moves to `tab.interaction.selection`
  per-tab (matching macOS, which already used per-tab state). macOS
  gains the `cancelPointerModes` call on tab-bar clicks that Windows
  has had since v0.3.x. Keyboard tab-switch (Cmd/Ctrl+N, prev/next)
  now cleans up *in-progress* drag state on the source tab while
  preserving *finished* highlights (β policy: matches iTerm2 /
  Terminal.app / WezTerm / Alacritty). Finished selections survive
  switching tabs and coming back; in-progress drags do not.
- **Rename auto-commit on every focus loss**
  ([#175](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/175)). The
  inline-rename spec is now uniform across mouse, keyboard shortcut,
  and F1 hide. Any focus-loss action ⇒ current input is committed as
  the new tab title. Only `Esc` cancels. Previously: Windows's
  `.shortcut` keyboard dispatch (Ctrl+N tab-switch, Ctrl+T new tab,
  Ctrl+W close, etc.) bypassed `commitRename`, leaving stale rename
  buffers behind. F1 hide ⇒ next show silently dropped the in-progress
  rename on both platforms. Same final UX as Finder filename rename,
  iTerm2 tab rename: pressing any modifier shortcut while renaming
  *applies* what you typed, doesn't throw it away. SPEC `§4.1 Rename
  focus loss 통합 표` documents every action's expected behavior.

## Known issues

- All v0.4.1 known issues still apply — see
  [`v0.4.1.md`](v0.4.1.md). No new ones from these fixes.

## Verification

- [ ] macOS: open two tabs. Click-and-drag in the terminal area
      selects text. Double-click selects the word and copies to
      clipboard. (v0.4.1 regression — fully silent in multi-tab mode.)
- [ ] Both platforms: a config with `"_note": "comment text"` at the
      top level *or* inside a nested object (e.g. inside `"font": {}`)
      loads without a fatal. Renaming `"shell"` to `"_shell"` falls
      back to the default shell, demonstrating the temporary-disable
      use.
- [ ] Both platforms: start a tab drag in tab A, switch to tab B with
      Cmd/Ctrl+N, come back to A — the half-finished drag is gone but
      A's finished selection from earlier (if any) is preserved.
- [ ] Both platforms: start renaming tab A, then press Ctrl/Cmd+T —
      A's typed name is applied as A's title, then a fresh tab opens
      and becomes active. Same with Ctrl/Cmd+W (close), Ctrl/Cmd+N
      (switch), F1 (hide). `Esc` while renaming still cancels and
      keeps the original title.
